subject: [PATCH v2 02/10] nfsd: fix UAF in async copy cancel and shutdown

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's async copy feature. The bug occurred when an async copy was freed or used after free while a teardown caller (such as OFFLOAD_CANCEL, nfsd4_shutdown_copy, or nfsd4_cancel_copy_by_sb) raced with the copy kthread. The patch fixes this issue by changing the lifetime of the async copy and its associated task_struct, ensuring that they are not freed until the kthread has completed its work. This is achieved through a combination of changes to the find_async_copy function, the nfsd4_do_async_copy function, and the nfsd4_send_cb_offload function. The patch also introduces a new reference counting mechanism for the async copy task_struct to prevent it from being freed prematurely.
